findings.detail: Only stdlib imports (re, unicodedata, dataclasses). No marketmind imports. Correct leaf utility.
SanitizedText used correctly by async_client.py (chat_flash, chat_pro) and macro_data.py. Both access .sanitized, .warnings, .truncated, .original_length fields.
All _INJECTION_PATTERNS use simple alternation (no nested quantifiers like (a+)+). _ZERO_WIDTH_CHARS uses explicit character listing. No catastrophic backtracking risk.

async_client.py uses source='llm_prompt' which is not a documented source type in sanitize_for_llm_prompt. Falls through to gate1_chat-equivalent behavior (rules 1,2,3,4). Not a bug, but source type should be documented or standardized.

notes: All 3 mandatory checks pass. One minor note about undocumented source type 'llm_prompt' — same behavior as 'gate1_chat', just undocumented.
